OBJECTIONS TO DEFT'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS IN SUPPORT OF MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF COOKE, THOMAS COOKE, CYNTHIA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE HAIRPIN TURN WAS A DANGEROUS CONDITION AND WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE DANGER. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE HAIRPIN TURN WAS A TRAP AND WHETHER DESIGN IMMUNITY APPLIES. LASTLY, THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING CAUSATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

ATTY. WARREN METLITZKY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3916 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE HAIRPIN TURN WAS A DANGEROUS CONDITION AND WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE DANGER. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE HAIRPIN TURN WAS A TRAP AND WHETHER DESIGN IMMUNITY APPLIES. LASTLY, THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING CAUSATION. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM
